thm:local-geometry.proof_tex:
Let \(\phi\) and \(\phi_b\) denote the \(N(0,1)\) and \(N(0,b)\) densities. By def:mirror-anm-paths,
\[p_{+,\delta}(x,y)=\phi(x)\phi_b\{y-\alpha x-\delta g(x)\},\qquad p_{-,\delta}(x,y)=p_{+,\delta}(y,x).	ag{1}\]
Ass:linear-center gives \(b=1-\alpha^2>0\), so these are positive \(C^2\) densities on all of \(\mathbb R^2\). At \(\delta=0\), (1) is the standardized bivariate Gaussian density with correlation \(\alpha\), hence both paths meet at \(P_0\). For \(\delta
e0\), the plus structural equations are \(X=Z\) and \(Y=\alpha Z+\delta g(Z)+E\), with \(E\perp Z\) and \(E\sim N(0,b)\); coordinate exchange gives the minus equations with \(Y\) as parent. Since \(g''\) is continuous and \(E[g''(Z)^2]>0\), it is not identically zero. Therefore \(f_\delta''=\delta g''\) is not identically zero for \(\delta
e0\), so these are nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANMs in the asserted directions.

Put \(r_\delta(x,y)=y-\alpha x-\delta g(x)\). Direct differentiation of (1) gives
\[J_y(P_{+,\delta})=-b^{-1},\qquad J_x(P_{+,\delta})=-1-b^{-1}(\alpha+\delta g'(x))^2+b^{-1}\delta r_\delta(x,y)g''(x).	ag{2}\]
Under \(P_{+,\delta}\), \((X,Y)=(Z,\alpha Z+\delta g(Z)+E)\), so \(r_\delta(X,Y)=E\). Since \(b+\alpha^2=1\), (2) becomes
\[J_x(P_{+,\delta})=-b^{-1}+\delta W_\delta,\qquad W_\delta=b^{-1}\{-2\alpha g'(Z)+E g''(Z)-\delta g'(Z)^2\}.	ag{3}\]
Coordinate exchange gives \(J_x(P_{-,\delta})=-b^{-1}\), while \(J_y(P_{-,\delta})\) has the distribution of the right side of (3). Consequently
\[V_y(P_{+,\delta})=V_x(P_{-,\delta})=0,\qquad V_x(P_{+,\delta})=V_y(P_{-,\delta})=\delta^2\operatorname{Var}(W_\delta).	ag{4}\]
Thus both maxima in the definition of \(C_m\) are equal and \(C_m(\delta)=\delta^2\operatorname{Var}(W_\delta)\). Ass:exponential-envelope supplies every polynomial moment of \(g'\) and \(g''\), in particular the fourth moment of \(g'\); independence and Gaussianity of \(E\) therefore imply \(W_\delta	o W_0\) in \(L^2\). Continuity of variance in \(L^2\) gives
\[C_m(\delta)=c_1\delta^2+o(\delta^2),\qquad c_1=\operatorname{Var}(W_0)=b^{-2}\operatorname{Var}\{2\alpha g'(Z)-E g''(Z)\}.	ag{5}\]
The conditional-variance identity yields
\[b^2c_1=E\{\operatorname{Var}(2\alpha g'(Z)-E g''(Z)\mid Z)\}+\operatorname{Var}\{E(2\alpha g'(Z)-E g''(Z)\mid Z)\}
=bE[g''(Z)^2]+4\alpha^2\operatorname{Var}(g'(Z))>0.	ag{6}\]
Hence \(c_1>0\), and continuity of \(\operatorname{Var}(W_\delta)\) makes \(C_m(\delta)>0\) for every sufficiently small \(\delta
e0\). All variances are finite by ass:exponential-envelope and every displayed division is valid because \(b>0\).

It remains to calculate the likelihood scale. Relative to \(P_0\), direct cancellation of the Gaussian densities gives the exact identities
\[L_{+,\delta}:= rac{p_{+,\delta}}{p_0}=\exp\left\{\delta v_+- rac{\delta^2g(X)^2}{2b}ight\},\qquad L_{-,\delta}:= rac{p_{-,\delta}}{p_0}=\exp\left\{\delta v_-- rac{\delta^2g(Y)^2}{2b}ight\}.	ag{7}\]
To verify quadratic-mean differentiability of the plus path, condition under \(P_0\) on \(X=x\), put \(W=(Y-\alpha X)/\sqrt b\sim N(0,1)\), and let \(u=\delta g(x)/\sqrt b\). Then \(\sqrt{L_{+,\delta}}=q_u(W):=\exp(uW/2-u^2/4)\). If \(F(u)=E[(q_u(W)-1-uW/2)^2]\), Gaussian integration gives
\[F(u)=2+u^2/4-2(1+u^2/4)e^{-u^2/8}=O(u^4),	ag{8}\]
and the elementary square inequality also gives
\[F(u)\le2E(q_u-1)^2+2E(uW/2)^2=4(1-e^{-u^2/8})+u^2/2\le u^2.	ag{9}\]
Therefore \(F(u)/u^2	o0\) and \(\delta^{-2}F(\delta g(x)/\sqrt b)\le g(x)^2/b\), whose right side is integrable by ass:exponential-envelope. Dominated convergence proves
\[\sqrt{L_{+,\delta}}=1+ rac{\delta}{2}v_++o_{L^2(P_0)}(\delta).	ag{10}\]
Conditioning on \(Y\) proves the analogous formula with \(v_-\). Direct conditional expectation gives \(E_0v_+=E_0v_-=0\); the envelope gives square integrability, so \(c_2<\infty\).

For \(t\in[0,1]\), set \(H_\delta(t)=\int p_{+,\delta}^t p_{-,\delta}^{1-t}\). Coordinate exchange gives \(H_\delta(t)=H_\delta(1-t)\), and Holder's inequality makes \(\log H_\delta\) convex. Its minimum is therefore at \(t=1/2\). Using (10),
\[1-\inf_tH_\delta(t)=1-\int\sqrt{p_{+,\delta}p_{-,\delta}}= rac12\|\sqrt{L_{+,\delta}}-\sqrt{L_{-,\delta}}\|_{L^2(P_0)}^2= rac{c_2}{8}\delta^2+o(\delta^2).	ag{11}\]
Because \(-\log(1-u)=u+o(u)\), def:chernoff-experiment gives
\[C_\star(\delta)= rac{c_2}{8}\delta^2+o(\delta^2).	ag{12}\]
To show strict separation, suppose \(c_2=0\). The continuous function \(v_+-v_-\) is then zero \(P_0\)-almost surely; since \(P_0\) has an everywhere-positive density, it vanishes everywhere. Thus
\[(y-\alpha x)g(x)=(x-\alpha y)g(y)\quad	ext{for every }(x,y)\in\mathbb R^2.	ag{13}\]
Putting \(y=\alpha x\) gives \(bxg(\alpha x)=0\) for all \(x\). Since \(b>0\), \(\alpha>0\), and \(g\) is continuous, this forces \(g\equiv0\), contradicting ass:nonlinear-curvature. Hence \(c_2>0\). Finally, (5) implies \(\delta^2=C_m(\delta)/c_1+o(C_m(\delta))\); substituting into (12) gives \(C_\star(\delta)=(c_2/(8c_1))C_m(\delta)+o(C_m(\delta))\). Ass:hermite-centering fixes the perturbation's constant and linear Gaussian components; no additional support, rank, positivity, overlap, or boundary condition is needed.

snapshot.stmt: For every sufficiently small delta != 0, P_{+,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM X->Y, P_{-,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM Y->X, V_y(P_{+,delta})=V_x(P_{-,delta})=0, and V_x(P_{+,delta})=V_y(P_{-,delta})=C_m(delta)>0. As delta -> 0, C_m(delta)=c_1 delta^2+o(delta^2), c_1>0, and C_star(delta)=(c_2/8)delta^2+o(delta^2)=(c_2/(8c_1))C_m(delta)+o(C_m(delta)), with c_2>0.

defs.def:score-variance: (s_P,J_j(P),V_j(P))=(grad log p,partial_j s_{P,j}(X,Y),Var_P[J_j(P)]) for every positive C^2 law P on R^2 and j in {x,y}.
defs.def:chernoff-experiment: R_n(delta)=inf_{pi in T_n} max_{h in {+,-}} P_{h,delta}^n{pi((X_i,Y_i)_{i=1}^n) != h} for every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0).

assumptions.ass:exponential-envelope: There exists eta > 0 such that E exp(eta(|g(Z)|+|g'(Z)|+|g''(Z)|)) < infinity.

thm:local-minimax-frontier.proof_tex:
Fix a sufficiently small \(\delta
e0\), abbreviate \(p=p_{+,\delta}\) and \(q=p_{-,\delta}\), and let \(S\) exchange the two coordinates. The mirror construction gives \(q(w)=p(Sw)\). By the symmetry and log-convexity calculation in thm:local-geometry,
\[A:=e^{-C_\star(\delta)}=\int_{\mathbb R^2}\sqrt{pq}<1.	ag{1}\]
Define \(m=A^{-1}\sqrt{pq}\) and \(\ell=\log(p/q)\). Positivity of the Gaussian densities makes both well defined. Coordinate exchange leaves \(m\) invariant and sends \(\ell\) to \(-\ell\). Moreover \(E_m\ell^2<\infty\): with \(u=p/q\), the boundedness of \(\sqrt u(\log u)^2/(1+u)\) on \((0,\infty)\) gives \(E_m\ell^2\le2K/A\). Thus \(E_m\ell=0\); write \(\sigma^2=E_m\ell^2\).

Let \(B_n= rac12\int\min(p^n,q^n)\), the equal-prior Bayes error, and let \(S_n=\sum_{i=1}^n\ell(X_i,Y_i)\) under \(m^n\). Since \(p=Ame^{\ell/2}\) and \(q=Ame^{-\ell/2}\),
\[B_n= rac{A^n}{2}E_{m^n}e^{-|S_n|/2}.	ag{2}\]
The expectation is at most one. If \(\sigma>0\), Chebyshev's inequality gives \(m^n\{|S_n|\le2\sigma\sqrt n\}\ge3/4\); if \(\sigma=0\), then \(S_n=0\) almost surely. Hence in either case
\[ rac38A^ne^{-\sigma\sqrt n}\le B_n\le rac12A^n,\qquad -n^{-1}\log B_n\longrightarrow C_\star(\delta).	ag{3}\]

Mirror symmetry makes this Bayes error exactly minimax. Take the likelihood-ratio rule, choosing \(+\) on \(p^n>q^n\), \(-\) on \(p^n<q^n\), and on equality use any Borel label antisymmetric under \(S\) off the fixed-point set. Such a label is obtained by inspecting the first sample coordinate with \(x_i
e y_i\); the fixed-point set \(x_i=y_i\) for every \(i\) is Lebesgue-null. Changing variables by \(S\) shows that the rule's two errors are equal. Their average is \(B_n\), so its maximum error is \(B_n\). Conversely, every rule's maximum error is at least its equal-prior average error, and that average is at least \(B_n\). Therefore \(R_n(\delta)=B_n\). Combining this identity with (3) proves
\[R_n(\delta)=\exp\{-n[C_\star(\delta)+o_n(1)]\}.	ag{4}\]
For fixed \(\delta
e0\), \(C_m(\delta)>0\) by thm:local-geometry, so
\[\lim_{n	o\infty} rac{-\log R_n(\delta)}{nC_m(\delta)}= rac{C_\star(\delta)}{C_m(\delta)}.\]
Taking the outer limit \(\delta\downarrow0\) and invoking thm:local-geometry yields \(c_2/(8c_1)\). This is the stated iterated, pointwise mirror-path limit.

snapshot.stmt: For each fixed sufficiently small delta != 0, R_n(delta)=exp{-n[C_star(delta)+o_n(1)]} as n -> infinity. Consequently, lim_(delta downarrow 0) lim_(n -> infinity) [-log R_n(delta)]/[n C_m(delta)] = c_2/(8c_1), so the optimal binary order-testing exponent in the mirror experiment is linear in C_m(delta).

defs.def:chernoff-experiment: R_n(delta)=inf_{pi in T_n} max_{h in {+,-}} P_{h,delta}^n{pi((X_i,Y_i)_{i=1}^n) != h} for every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0).

assumptions.ass:exponential-envelope: There exists eta > 0 such that E exp(eta(|g(Z)|+|g'(Z)|+|g''(Z)|)) < infinity.

thm:local-minimax-elbow.proof_tex:
Write \(S(x,y)=(y,x)\) and extend \(S\) coordinatewise to \(n\) observations. The mirror construction gives, pointwise,
\[p_{-,\delta}(x,y)=p_{+,\delta}(S(x,y)),\qquad \ell_\delta(S(x,y))=-\ell_\delta(x,y).	ag{1}\]
All densities are positive because \(b=1-\alpha^2>0\). The likelihood-ratio rule is therefore Borel. Away from the likelihood tie set it is antisymmetric under \(S\), and on the tie set def:mirror-equalizer-rule makes it antisymmetric except on \(F_n=\{x_i=y_i	ext{ for every }i\}\). The set \(F_n\) is Lebesgue-null and hence \(P_{h,\delta}^n\)-null. A change of variables by the measure-preserving involution \(S\) yields
\[P_{+,\delta}^n\{\pi_{n,\delta}^{LR}=-\}=P_{-,\delta}^n\{\pi_{n,\delta}^{LR}=+\}.	ag{2}\]
Set
\[B_n(\delta)= rac12\inf_{\pi\in T_n}\left[P_{+,\delta}^n\{\pi=-\}+P_{-,\delta}^n\{\pi=+\}ight]= rac12\int\min(p_{+,\delta}^n,p_{-,\delta}^n).	ag{3}\]
The likelihood-ratio rule attains the pointwise minimum in (3), including on ties. By (2), each of its errors equals their average \(B_n(\delta)\). The maximum error of any rule is at least its average error, which is at least \(B_n(\delta)\). Therefore, for every \((n,\delta)\),
\[R_n(\delta)=B_n(\delta)=P_{+,\delta}^n\{\pi_{n,\delta}^{LR}
e+\}=P_{-,\delta}^n\{\pi_{n,\delta}^{LR}
e-\}.	ag{4}\]
This includes \(\delta=0\): the laws coincide, and antisymmetric tie breaking splits their swap-invariant common law equally outside the null fixed-point set.

We next derive the local tilted moments. Relative to \(P_0\), thm:local-geometry established the exact identities
\[L_{+,\delta}= rac{p_{+,\delta}}{p_0}=\exp\left\{\delta v_+- rac{\delta^2g(X)^2}{2b}ight\},\qquad L_{-,\delta}= rac{p_{-,\delta}}{p_0}=\exp\left\{\delta v_-- rac{\delta^2g(Y)^2}{2b}ight\}.	ag{5}\]
Put
\[d=v_+-v_-,\qquad q= rac{g(Y)^2-g(X)^2}{2b}.	ag{6}\]
Then the one-observation log likelihood ratio has the exact expansion
\[\ell_\delta=\delta d+\delta^2q.	ag{7}\]
Let \(A_\delta=\int\sqrt{p_{+,\delta}p_{-,\delta}}\) and \(M_\delta=A_\delta^{-1}\sqrt{L_{+,\delta}L_{-,\delta}}\), so \(M_\delta=dm_\delta/dP_0\). Mirror symmetry makes \(m_\delta\) invariant under \(S\), while (1) makes \(\ell_\delta\) antisymmetric. Hence
\[E_{m_\delta}\ell_\delta=0.	ag{8}\]
The integrability behind this identity and the following limits is explicit. Under \(P_0\), let \(a=Y-\alpha X\) and \(c=X-\alpha Y\). Completing squares in (5) gives
\[\sqrt{L_{+,\delta}L_{-,\delta}}=\exp\left\{ rac{\delta a g(X)+\delta c g(Y)}{2b}- rac{\delta^2(g(X)^2+g(Y)^2)}{4b}ight\}\le\exp\left\{ rac{a^2+c^2}{4b}ight\}=:H.	ag{9}\]
The Gaussian vector \((a,c)\) has covariance \(b egin{pmatrix}1&-\alpha\-\alpha&1\end{pmatrix}\), whose largest eigenvalue is \(b(1+\alpha)\). Therefore \(E_0H^r<\infty\) for every \(1<r<2/(1+\alpha)\), a nonempty interval because \(0<\alpha<1\). Ass:exponential-envelope gives all polynomial moments of \(g(X),g(Y)\), and Gaussianity gives all polynomial moments of \(a,c\). Holder's inequality with such an \(r>1\) consequently gives some \(\kappa\in(0,1]\) for which
\[E_0\left[H\{ |d|+\delta_0|q|\}^{2+\kappa}ight]<\infty.	ag{10}\]
Thm:local-geometry gives \(A_\delta=e^{-C_\star(\delta)}	o1\). Equations (7), (9), and (10), followed by dominated convergence, imply
\[E_{m_\delta}\ell_\delta^2=\delta^2E_0\left[M_\delta(d+\delta q)^2ight]=\delta^2\{E_0d^2+o(1)\}=c_2\delta^2+o(\delta^2),	ag{11}\]
and also
\[\sup_{0<|\delta|<\delta_1}E_{m_\delta}|\ell_\delta/\delta|^{2+\kappa}<\infty	ag{12}\]
for some \(\delta_1>0\). Thus, along every \(\delta_n\downarrow0\), \(E_{m_n}\ell_{\delta_n}^2=O(\delta_n^2)\), as asserted.

The exact minimax risk may now be written under the tilted law. Symmetry places the Chernoff minimum at \(1/2\), so \(A_\delta=e^{-C_\star(\delta)}\). With \(L_n=\sum_{i=1}^n\ell_{\delta_n}(X_i,Y_i)\), the identities \(p_{+,\delta_n}=A_nm_ne^{\ell_{\delta_n}/2}\) and \(p_{-,\delta_n}=A_nm_ne^{-\ell_{\delta_n}/2}\), together with (3)-(4), give
\[R_n(\delta_n)= rac{A_n^n}{2}E_{m_n^n}e^{-|L_n|/2}= rac{e^{-nC_\star(\delta_n)}}2E_{m_n^n}e^{-|L_n|/2}.	ag{13}\]

Suppose first that \(nC_m(\delta_n)	oho\in[0,\infty)\). By thm:local-geometry,
\[n\delta_n^2\longrightarrow\lambda:=ho/c_1,\qquad nC_\star(\delta_n)\longrightarrow	au^2/8,\qquad 	au^2:=c_2\lambda=ho c_2/c_1.	ag{14}\]
If \(ho>0\), (8), (11), and (12) give \(L_n\Rightarrow N(0,	au^2)\) under \(m_n^n\). To see this without invoking an undeclared limit theorem, for fixed \(t\) use \(|e^{iu}-1-iu+u^2/2|\le K_\kappa|u|^{2+\kappa}\) with (8) and (12):
\[E_{m_n}e^{it\ell_{\delta_n}}=1- rac{t^2}{2}E_{m_n}\ell_{\delta_n}^2+O(\delta_n^{2+\kappa}).	ag{15}\]
Here \(n\delta_n^{2+\kappa}	o0\), while (11) and (14) give \(nE_{m_n}\ell_{\delta_n}^2	o	au^2\); taking the \(n\)-th power in (15) proves the Gaussian convergence. Since \(z\mapsto e^{-|z|/2}\) is bounded and continuous, (13)-(14) imply, for \(G\sim N(0,	au^2)\),
\[R_n(\delta_n)\longrightarrow rac{e^{-	au^2/8}}2E e^{-|G|/2}=e^{-	au^2/8}E[e^{G/2}1\{G<0\}]=\Phi(-	au/2).	ag{16}\]
The last equality follows by completing the square in the normal density. If \(ho=0\), (11) gives \(E_{m_n^n}L_n^2=nE_{m_n}\ell_{\delta_n}^2	o0\), so \(L_n	o0\) in probability, while (14) gives \(nC_\star(\delta_n)	o0\). Equation (13) then gives \(R_n(\delta_n)	o1/2=\Phi(0)\). Therefore
\[R_n(\delta_n)\longrightarrow\Phi\left(- rac12\sqrt{ho c_2/c_1}ight).	ag{17}\]

Finally suppose \(nC_m(\delta_n)	o\infty\). Thm:local-geometry implies \(N_n:=nC_\star(\delta_n)	o\infty\), \(N_n\asymp n\delta_n^2\), and \(C_\star(\delta_n)/C_m(\delta_n)	o c_2/(8c_1)\). By (8) and (11),
\[\operatorname{Var}_{m_n^n}(L_n)=nE_{m_n}\ell_{\delta_n}^2=O(N_n).	ag{18}\]
Take \(a_n=N_n^{3/4}\). Chebyshev's inequality gives \(m_n^n\{|L_n|\le a_n\}=1-o(1)\). Equation (13) therefore yields
\[ rac{e^{-N_n}}2e^{-a_n/2}\{1-o(1)\}\le R_n(\delta_n)\le rac{e^{-N_n}}2.	ag{19}\]
Because \(a_n=o(N_n)\), taking negative logarithms proves
\[ rac{-\log R_n(\delta_n)}{nC_\star(\delta_n)}\longrightarrow1.	ag{20}\]
Multiplying (20) by \(C_\star(\delta_n)/C_m(\delta_n)	o c_2/(8c_1)\) gives
\[ rac{-\log R_n(\delta_n)}{nC_m(\delta_n)}\longrightarrow rac{c_2}{8c_1}.	ag{21}\]
All constants belong to the one fixed mirror path. No uniform lower envelope over a global nonlinear-ANM class is asserted.

snapshot.stmt: For every (n,delta), the antisymmetrically tie-broken likelihood-ratio rule pi_{n,delta}^LR satisfies R_n(delta)=P_{+,delta}^n{pi_{n,delta}^LR != +}=P_{-,delta}^n{pi_{n,delta}^LR != -}. Along every delta_n downarrow 0, E_{m_n}[ell_{delta_n}(X,Y)^2]=O(delta_n^2). If n C_m(delta_n)->rho in [0,infinity), then R_n(delta_n)->Phi(-0.5 sqrt(rho c_2/c_1)); if n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, then [-log R_n(delta_n)]/[n C_star(delta_n)]->1 and [-log R_n(delta_n)]/[n C_m(delta_n)]->c_2/(8c_1). Both limits are pointwise for the fixed admissible mirror path.

defs.def:mirror-equalizer-rule: For w=((x_i,y_i))_{i=1}^n, let mathsf S_n w=((y_i,x_i))_{i=1}^n and choose a Borel a_n with a_n(mathsf S_n w)=-a_n(w) off the fixed-point set. For every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0), set pi_{n,delta}^LR(w)=+ if product_{i=1}^n p_{+,delta}(x_i,y_i)>product_{i=1}^n p_{-,delta}(x_i,y_i), set pi_{n,delta}^LR(w)=- if the inequality is reversed, and set pi_{n,delta}^LR(w)=a_n(w) on equality.
defs.def:chernoff-experiment: R_n(delta)=inf_{pi in T_n} max_{h in {+,-}} P_{h,delta}^n{pi((X_i,Y_i)_{i=1}^n) != h} for every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0).

assumptions.ass:exponential-envelope: There exists eta > 0 such that E exp(eta(|g(Z)|+|g'(Z)|+|g''(Z)|)) < infinity.

prop:score-contrast-template.proof_tex:
Fix a sequence \(\delta_n\downarrow0\) satisfying \(nC_m(\delta_n)	o\infty\), and write \(P_{h,n}=P_{h,\delta_n}\). All claims are pointwise along this mirror path. The differentiation in thm:local-geometry gives, under \(P_{+,\delta}\),
\[J_y(P_{+,\delta})=-b^{-1},\qquad J_x(P_{+,\delta})=-b^{-1}+\delta W_\delta,	ag{1}\]
where \(W_\delta=b^{-1}[-2\alpha g'(Z)+E g''(Z)-\delta g'(Z)^2]\). Under \(P_{-,\delta}\), coordinate exchange gives \(J_x=-b^{-1}\) and \(J_y=-b^{-1}+\delta W_\delta^-\), where \(W_\delta^-(x,y)=W_\delta(y,x)\) has the same law as \(W_\delta\). In particular, the leaf entry is constant and the non-leaf entry has variance
\[\delta^2\operatorname{Var}(W_\delta)=C_m(\delta).	ag{2}\]

For arbitrary \(U,e\in L^2(P)\), let \(U^\circ=U-E_PU\) and \(e^\circ=e-E_Pe\). Expanding the centered square gives
\[\operatorname{Var}_P(U+e)-\operatorname{Var}_P(U)=2E_P(U^\circ e^\circ)+E_P[(e^\circ)^2].	ag{3}\]
Cauchy-Schwarz proves
\[|\operatorname{Var}_P(U+e)-\operatorname{Var}_P(U)|\le2\sqrt{\operatorname{Var}_P(U)\operatorname{Var}_P(e)}+\operatorname{Var}_P(e).	ag{4}\]
Condition on the training observations for a fixed fold. The fitted error \(e=\widehat J-J\) is then a fixed measurable function of an independent evaluation draw. Ass:jacobian-estimability gives \(\|e\|_{L^4(P_{h,n})}=O_P(r_n)\), hence
\[\operatorname{Var}_{P_{h,n}}(e)\le\|e\|_{L^2(P_{h,n})}^2\le\|e\|_{L^4(P_{h,n})}^2=O_P(r_n^2).	ag{5}\]
For a non-leaf entry, (2) and thm:local-geometry give \(\sqrt{\operatorname{Var}(U)}=\sqrt{C_m(\delta_n)}=O(|\delta_n|)\), so (4)-(5) give a conditional centered-variance change \(O_P(|\delta_n|r_n+r_n^2)\). For a leaf entry, \(U\) is constant and (3) reduces to \(\operatorname{Var}(U+e)=\operatorname{Var}(e)=O_P(r_n^2)\). This proves the population perturbation assertions.

It remains to control the empirical fold variances. Let \(N_{n,k}=|I_{n,k}|\); both tend to infinity. For numbers \(a_1,\ldots,a_N\), write \(\mathbb V_N(a)=N^{-1}\sum_i(a_i- ar a)^2\). The sample analogue of (4) is the deterministic inequality
\[|\mathbb V_N(u+e)-\mathbb V_N(u)|\le2\{\mathbb V_N(u)\mathbb V_N(e)\}^{1/2}+\mathbb V_N(e).	ag{6}\]
Ass:exponential-envelope and the exact formula for \(W_\delta\) give \(\sup_{|\delta|\le\delta_1}E|W_\delta|^4<\infty\). Hence the triangular weak law, applied to \(W_{\delta_n}\) and \(W_{\delta_n}^2\), yields on each evaluation fold
\[\mathbb V_{N_{n,k}}(W_{\delta_n})=\operatorname{Var}(W_{\delta_n})+o_P(1).	ag{7}\]
Indeed, the variance of each empirical first or second moment is uniformly \(O(N_{n,k}^{-1})\), so Chebyshev's inequality applies. Thus the oracle empirical leaf variance is zero and the oracle empirical non-leaf variance is
\[\delta_n^2\mathbb V_{N_{n,k}}(W_{\delta_n})=C_m(\delta_n)+o_P(\delta_n^2)=C_m(\delta_n)+o_P(C_m(\delta_n)).	ag{8}\]
For the empirical nuisance term, conditional on training,
\[E\left[N_{n,k}^{-1}\sum_{i\in I_{n,k}}e(X_i,Y_i)^2\mid	ext{training}ight]=\|e\|_{L^2(P_{h,n})}^2.	ag{9}\]
To make the training-random order explicit, for every \(\varepsilon>0\) choose \(C<\infty\) so that \(P\{\|e\|_4>Cr_n\}<\varepsilon\) eventually. On the complementary event, conditional Markov inequality bounds the probability that the average in (9) exceeds \(LC^2r_n^2\) by \(L^{-1}\). Letting \(L\) be large shows
\[\mathbb V_{N_{n,k}}(e)\le N_{n,k}^{-1}\sum_{i\in I_{n,k}}e(X_i,Y_i)^2=O_P(r_n^2).	ag{10}\]
Substitution of (7) and (10) into (6) proves, simultaneously over the two coordinates and two folds, that fitting changes a non-leaf empirical variance by \(O_P(|\delta_n|r_n+r_n^2)\) and a leaf empirical variance by \(O_P(r_n^2)\). Reciprocal two-fold cross-fitting causes no problem: each marginal fold argument conditions only on that fold's fitted functions, which are independent of its evaluation observations, and a union bound joins the four cases.

By thm:local-geometry, \(C_m(\delta_n)=c_1\delta_n^2+o(\delta_n^2)\) with \(c_1>0\). Thus \(r_n=o(\sqrt{C_m(\delta_n)})\) is equivalent along this path to \(r_n=o(|\delta_n|)\), and therefore
\[|\delta_n|r_n+r_n^2=o(C_m(\delta_n)).	ag{11}\]
Equations (8)-(11), averaged over the two folds as in def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template, give
\[ rac{\widehat\Delta_n}{C_m(\delta_n)}\xrightarrow[P_{+,n}^n]{P}1,\qquad  rac{\widehat\Delta_n}{C_m(\delta_n)}\xrightarrow[P_{-,n}^n]{P}-1.	ag{12}\]
The denominator \(\widehat\sigma_n\vee n^{-1}\) is strictly positive, so \(\widehat Z_n\) and \(\widehat\Delta_n\) have the same sign. The template declares \(D_n=+\) exactly when \(\widehat Z_n\ge0\). Since \(C_m(\delta_n)>0\) eventually, (12) proves
\[P_{+,\delta_n}^n(D_n
e+)	o0,\qquad P_{-,\delta_n}^n(D_n
e-)	o0.\]
Taking the maximum proves the proposition in the declared supercritical regime \(nC_m(\delta_n)	o\infty\). Thm:local-minimax-frontier is used only to locate this sign-consistency result relative to the likelihood benchmark; no likelihood exponent for the template is claimed.

snapshot.stmt: Under ass:jacobian-estimability, the following conditional score-contrast result holds. Under P_{+,delta}, J_y(P_{+,delta})=-b^(-1) and J_x(P_{+,delta})=-b^(-1)+delta W_delta; under P_{-,delta} the two identities are swapped. For every U,e in L^2(P), |Var_P(U+e)-Var_P(U)| <= 2 sqrt(Var_P(U) Var_P(e))+Var_P(e). Hence, conditionally on each training fold, an L4 nuisance error e with ||e||_{L4(P_{h,delta_n})}=O_P(r_n) changes the non-leaf centered variance by O_P(|delta_n|r_n+r_n^2) and the leaf centered variance by O_P(r_n^2). For every instantiation D_n of def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template using the common rate realizers in ass:jacobian-estimability, if delta_n downarrow 0, n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, and r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))), then max_{h in {+,-}}P_{h,delta_n}^n{D_n != h}->0.

defs.def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template: For n>=2 and any two-fold cross-fitted L4 rate realizers widehat J_{j,k} returned by the same h/P-independent data-measurable training mappings in ass:jacobian-estimability, let I_{n,1}={1,...,floor(n/2)} and I_{n,2}={floor(n/2)+1,...,n}. For each k in {1,2}, train widehat J_{j,k} on the observations outside I_{n,k}; set widehat V_{j,k}=|I_{n,k}|^{-1} sum_{i in I_{n,k}}[widehat J_{j,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{j,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2, widehat Delta_n=2^{-1}sum_{k=1}^2(widehat V_{x,k}-widehat V_{y,k}), widehat sigma_n^2=2^{-1}sum_{k=1}^2|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{i in I_{n,k}}( [widehat J_{x,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{x,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2-[widehat J_{y,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{y,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2-widehat Delta_n)^2, widehat Z_n=sqrt(n)widehat Delta_n/(widehat sigma_n vee n^{-1}), and D_n=+ if widehat Z_n>=0 and D_n=- otherwise.
defs.def:chernoff-experiment: R_n(delta)=inf_{pi in T_n} max_{h in {+,-}} P_{h,delta}^n{pi((X_i,Y_i)_{i=1}^n) != h} for every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0).

assumptions.ass:jacobian-estimability: For every n>=2, k in {1,2}, and j in {x,y}, the same single data-measurable map of the observations outside I_{n,k}, independent of h and of the unknown observational law P, returns widehat J_{j,k}; its L4(P_{h,delta_n}) error for J_j(P_{h,delta_n}) is O_{P_{h,delta_n}^n}(r_n), uniformly over (h,j,k) in {+,-} x {x,y} x {1,2}.
assumptions.ass:exponential-envelope: There exists eta > 0 such that E exp(eta(|g(Z)|+|g'(Z)|+|g''(Z)|)) < infinity.

prop:linear-reduction.proof_tex:
Fix any integer $n\ge 1$. By $0<\alpha<1$ in ass:linear-center, $b=1-\alpha^2>0$. At $\delta=0$, $f_0(z)=\alpha z$, so def:mirror-anm-paths gives $P_{+,0}=\operatorname{Law}(Z,\alpha Z+E)=P_0$. The density of this law is
\[p_{+,0}(x,y)= rac{1}{2\pi\sqrt b}\exp\left\{- rac{x^2}{2}- rac{(y-\alpha x)^2}{2b}ight\}.\]
The reverse construction gives $P_{-,0}=\operatorname{Law}(\alpha Z+E,Z)$, whose density is
\[p_{-,0}(x,y)= rac{1}{2\pi\sqrt b}\exp\left\{- rac{y^2}{2}- rac{(x-\alpha y)^2}{2b}ight\}.\]
Since $b=1-\alpha^2$, direct expansion yields
\[b x^2+(y-\alpha x)^2=x^2+y^2-2\alpha xy=b y^2+(x-\alpha y)^2.\]
Consequently $p_{+,0}(x,y)=p_{-,0}(x,y)$ for every $(x,y)$, and hence $P_{+,0}=P_{-,0}=P_0$.

It remains to evaluate the minimax risk in def:chernoff-experiment. For any $\pi\in T_n$, put $q=P_0^n\{\pi=+\}$. Because the two hypotheses induce the same sample law,
\[P_{+,0}^n\{\pi
e +\}=1-q,\qquad P_{-,0}^n\{\pi
e -\}=q.\]
Thus $\max\{1-q,q\}\ge 1/2$, and taking the infimum over $\pi$ shows $R_n(0)\ge 1/2$. For the reverse inequality, define the Borel rule $\pi^*((x_i,y_i)_{i=1}^n)=+$ when $x_1\ge0$ and $\pi^*=-$ when $x_1<0$. Under $P_0^n$, $X_1=Z_1\sim N(0,1)$, so $P_0^n\{\pi^*=+\}=P_0^n\{\pi^*=-\}=1/2$. Therefore both error probabilities of $\pi^*$ equal $1/2$, giving $R_n(0)\le1/2$. Combining the bounds proves $R_n(0)=1/2$ for every $n$.

snapshot.stmt: At delta=0, P_{+,0}=P_{-,0}=P_0 and R_n(0)=1/2 for every n.

defs.def:chernoff-experiment: R_n(delta)=inf_{pi in T_n} max_{h in {+,-}} P_{h,delta}^n{pi((X_i,Y_i)_{i=1}^n) != h} for every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0).

oeq:primitive-tilted-attainability.proof_tex: The exact bookkeeping identities are P_{+,delta_n}^n(D_n=-)=exp(-n C_star(delta_n))K_{+,n} and P_{-,delta_n}^n(D_n=+)=exp(-n C_star(delta_n))K_{-,n}. Hence the requested condition is sufficient exactly when both tilted losses are exp(o(n C_star(delta_n))). Moreover, the frozen L4 premise is insufficient: for the admissible rare-event perturbation, P_{+,delta_n}^n(D_n=-)>=q_n^2, so log K_{+,n}>=n C_star(delta_n)+2 log q_n=n C_star(delta_n)-o(n C_star(delta_n)), which contradicts log K_{+,n}=o(n C_star(delta_n)). The strongest positive result available from the frozen primitives remains the conditional sign consistency in prop:score-contrast-template when r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))).

snapshot.stmt: Which primitive, verifiable conditions on a concrete two-fold nuisance-training procedure imply log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)) along an admissible sequence delta_n downarrow 0 with n C_m(delta_n)->infinity?

defs.def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template: For n>=2 and any two-fold cross-fitted L4 rate realizers widehat J_{j,k} returned by the same h/P-independent data-measurable training mappings in ass:jacobian-estimability, let I_{n,1}={1,...,floor(n/2)} and I_{n,2}={floor(n/2)+1,...,n}. For each k in {1,2}, train widehat J_{j,k} on the observations outside I_{n,k}; set widehat V_{j,k}=|I_{n,k}|^{-1} sum_{i in I_{n,k}}[widehat J_{j,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{j,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2, widehat Delta_n=2^{-1}sum_{k=1}^2(widehat V_{x,k}-widehat V_{y,k}), widehat sigma_n^2=2^{-1}sum_{k=1}^2|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{i in I_{n,k}}( [widehat J_{x,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{x,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2-[widehat J_{y,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{y,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2-widehat Delta_n)^2, widehat Z_n=sqrt(n)widehat Delta_n/(widehat sigma_n vee n^{-1}), and D_n=+ if widehat Z_n>=0 and D_n=- otherwise.
defs.def:score-contrast-exponent-handle: For every admissible delta_n downarrow 0 with n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, set A_n=integral sqrt(p_{+,delta_n}p_{-,delta_n})=exp(-C_star(delta_n)), m_n=A_n^(-1)sqrt(p_{+,delta_n}p_{-,delta_n}), ell_delta=log(p_{+,delta}/p_{-,delta}), L_n=sum_(i=1)^n ell_{delta_n}(X_i,Y_i), K_{+,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(L_n/2)1{widehat Delta_n<0}], and K_{-,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(-L_n/2)1{widehat Delta_n>=0}]. Handle: search for primitive, verifiable conditions on a concrete nuisance-training procedure that make log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)), using the returned-function sigma-field G_n rather than an evaluation-independent training field.
defs.def:rare-event-nuisance-perturbation: For an admissible delta_n downarrow 0 satisfying n C_star(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity, set q_n=1/log(n+e) and fix a positive deterministic amplitude schedule M_n up-arrow infinity; both schedules are independent of h, delta_n, and the unknown law P. For each k in {1,2}, select one distinct observation i(k) outside I_{n,k} and set A_{n,k}={X_{i(k)}>Phi^{-1}(1-q_n)}. The A_{n,k} are independent under P_{+,delta_n} and each has probability q_n; under P_{-,delta_n}, each probability tends to zero by uniform local tightness and the diverging threshold. Starting from any common h/P-independent data-measurable rate-realizing maps widehat J_{j,k}, set tilde J_{x,k}=widehat J_{x,k} and tilde J_{y,k}=widehat J_{y,k} off A_{n,k}; on A_{n,k}, set tilde J_{x,k}=0 and tilde J_{y,k}=M_n x. The perturbed maps are the same common h/P-independent data-measurable mappings under both hypotheses. Insert (tilde J_{x,k},tilde J_{y,k}) into def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template.

assumptions.ass:jacobian-estimability: For every n>=2, k in {1,2}, and j in {x,y}, the same single data-measurable map of the observations outside I_{n,k}, independent of h and of the unknown observational law P, returns widehat J_{j,k}; its L4(P_{h,delta_n}) error for J_j(P_{h,delta_n}) is O_{P_{h,delta_n}^n}(r_n), uniformly over (h,j,k) in {+,-} x {x,y} x {1,2}.
